title: Top-K Token Filter
description: Implement the top-k filtering step used before sampling the next token from a language model.
problem: Write top_k(probs, k) where probs is a dict mapping token->probability. Return a new dict containing only the k tokens with the highest probabilities, unchanged values.
hint: Use sorted(probs.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:k], then build a dict from that.
